title: Sophisticated vs. Naive: When the Extra Depth Pays

runtime.expected_outcome: On open mazes (forked_paths) naive enumeration and sophisticated plan match. On adversarial mazes (deceptive) sophisticated wins.

symbols.naive plan: expectation under a fixed future policy
symbols.sophisticated plan: min under the best future policy

audiences.kid: Thinking harder about what you'll do next means you pick a better move now -- but only in tricky situations.
audiences.real: Sophisticated inference is a policy-quality improvement in adversarial or sparse-reward settings; on easy layouts the extra compute is wasted.
audiences.equation: Sophisticated >= naive in the min-at-each-step sense; equality when the agent's best future plan equals its average future plan.
audiences.derivation: The min operator lower-bounds the expectation, so sophisticated EFE <= naive EFE, making the sophisticated policy at least as good (Friston et al. 2021).

orchestrate.objective: Quantify when sophisticated planning pays off vs. the extra compute cost.
orchestrate.context: Run both Plan and SophisticatedPlan on forked_paths and deceptive_dead_end; log compute + episode length.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), App B.
Friston, Da Costa, Hafner, Hesp & Parr (2021), Sophisticated inference.
